description_long: This is a well-worn axe featuring a robust, rusted metal head and a weathered wooden handle. The axe head is broad and wedge-shaped, with a slightly convex cutting edge and a flat poll, showing signs of oxidation and use. The handle is made of light-colored wood, displaying a gentle curve for ergonomic grip and a visibly smoothed surface from repeated handling. The junction between the head and handle is traditional, with the head fitted securely onto the handle, likely wedged in place. The overall appearance suggests this tool has seen extensive use, with discoloration and patina on both the metal and wood. This axe would be commonly found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance sheds.
description: A weathered axe with a rusted metal head and a curved, light wooden handle, showing signs of heavy use. The tool has a broad blade and a traditional, ergonomic design.
description_view_0: The axe is viewed from the top of the blade, showing the narrow profile of the head and the alignment of the handle.
description_view_1: The axe is seen from the side, highlighting the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, rusted blade.
description_view_2: The axe is viewed from the handle end, displaying the rounded butt and the straight alignment toward the blade.
description_view_3: The opposite side view reveals the other face of the blade and handle, emphasizing the weathered texture and patina.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle, oval cross-section, smoothed from use, ergonomic grip, light color, traditional style.
attribute_blade_design: Broad wedge-shaped steel blade, single cutting edge, flat poll, convex edge profile, no beard, rusted surface.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting, blade fitted onto handle with visible swelling at the joint.
